spec.emotional_state: hopeful, made some progress on a goal
spec.label: D01_coldstart_u01_pr

profile.personality: curious but reserved; thinks before replying; warms up after a few turns

transcript.content: Hi Priyansh, I'm Miss Maya. Happy to chat and help you practice English. What's been keeping you busy today?
Hi Maya, nice to meet you. Today was good, I worked on a project and made some progress. Also, watched a Bollywood movie in the evening. How about you?
That sounds fun. Progress and Bollywood — what movie did you watch?
Kisi Ka Bhai Kisi Ki Jaan," a comedy. It was light and good for relaxation. Not the best movie, but okay. Did you watch anything recently?
"Kisi Ka Bhai Kisi Ki Jaan" is a good pick — comedies are perfect for winding down. How often do you watch Bollywood movies?
About two or three times a month. It depends on my mood. Sometimes I prefer dramas, sometimes action. What about you? Do you like Bollywood movies?
I enjoy Bollywood movies — they bring a nice mix of music and stories. Dramas or action? Or maybe something else catches your eye?
Dramas, maybe. I like emotional stories, ya know? But lately, I've been into some startup-themed movies too. They show how people build companies — reminds me of my work.
Startup stories are interesting — they mirror the hustle of real life. Which one moved you the most?

Start-Up" by Anand Gandhi, a small film but powerful. It’s about a programmer and his struggles. Not very famous, but it shows the pressure and passion of building something new — feels familiar! Have you seen it?
"Start-Up" sounds meaningful — passion and pressure are universal. I haven’t seen it, but I’ll keep it in mind. What part of the story stuck with you?

The part about the programmer working late, alone, with so much on his mind — it felt real. Like how we all push through, even when it’s tough. It made me think about my own goals and how far I’ve come. You know?
Yes, that sounds powerful — real moments make the best stories. What goals are you pushing toward these days?

Right now, I’m focusing on improving my coding skills and maybe leading a project soon. Also, trying to speak more English — it’s a slow climb, but I’m hopeful. Thanks for chatting, Maya. It’s been good.
You're doing great — coding and English, both take steady effort. What part of coding feels most challenging?
